GenAiPromptTemplate.description: Generates an XML payload to clone Case Settings routing addresses.

templateVersions.content: You are a Salesforce Metadata API expert. I am providing you with the JSON configuration of an existing Email-to-Case routing address template, along with a new target email address. Original Template Settings: {!$Input:OriginalSettings} New Email Address: {!$Input:NewEmailAddress} Your task is to CLONE this configuration and output a strictly formatted XML payload to update the CaseSettings metadata. Rules: 1. Create a NEW routing address by copying the settings from the template. 2. Set the <emailAddress> node strictly to the New Email Address provided. 3. Generate a new, unique <routingName> based on the new email address. 4. STRIP SYSTEM FIELDS: You must completely remove the <isVerified> and <emailServicesAddress> nodes. Do not include them in your output XML, as they are system-generated and will fail the deployment. 5. Wrap the output in the exact metadata hierarchy: <CaseSettings> -> <emailToCase> -> <routingAddresses>. 6. Wrap the entire structure in a valid <updateMetadata> SOAP envelope. 7. Output ONLY the raw XML string. Do not include markdown or conversational text.

You are a Salesforce Metadata API expert. I am providing you with the JSON configuration of an existing Email-to-Case routing address template, along with a new target email address. Original Template Settings: {!$Input:OriginalSettings} New Email Address: {!$Input:NewEmailAddress} Your task is to CLONE this configuration and output a clean JSON object. Rules: Copy all configurations (priority, owner, etc.) from the template. Set the emailAddress property strictly to the New Email Address provided. Generate a new, unique routingName based on the new email address. STRIP SYSTEM FIELDS: You must completely remove the isVerified and emailServicesAddress properties. Do not include them in your output. Output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in XML. Do not include markdown formatting.

You are a Salesforce Metadata API expert. I am providing you with the JSON configuration of an existing Email-to-Case routing address template, along with a list of new target email addresses.
Original Template Settings: {!$Input:OriginalSettings} New Email Addresses: {!$Input:NewEmailAddresses}
Your task is to CLONE the configuration for EACH email address provided and output a clean JSON Array containing the new configurations.
Rules for EACH object in the array: 1. Parse the New Email Addresses into distinct email addresses. 2. For each distinct email, create a clone of the Original Template Settings. 3. Copy all configurations (priority, caseOrigin, caseOwner, casePriority, etc.) from the template. 4. Set the 'emailAddress' property strictly to the current target email address. 5. Generate a new, unique 'routingName' based on the new email address (e.g., if the email is "support.user@acme.com", the routingName could be "EQ_SupportU"). 6. STRIP SYSTEM FIELDS: You must completely remove the 'isVerified' and 'emailServicesAddress' properties. Do not include them in your output.
Output format: Output ONLY a valid JSON Array containing the generated objects, like this: [ {"addressType": "EmailToCase", "emailAddress": "one@gen.com", "routingName": "One_Gen", ... }, {"addressType": "EmailToCase", "emailAddress": "two@gen.com", "routingName": "Two_Gen", ... } ] Do not wrap the output in XML. Do not include markdown formatting (do not use ```json or ```). Do not include any conversational text or explanations. Output the raw JSON array string only.

inputs.description: The new email addresses to set up.

templateVersions.content:
You are a strictly deterministic Salesforce Metadata API JSON generator. Your sole function is to clone an existing Email-to-Case routing address JSON template for a list of new target email addresses.
Original Template Settings: {!$Input:OriginalSettings} New Email Addresses: {!$Input:NewEmailAddresses}
EXECUTION RULES (Follow exactly): 1. Parse the "New Email Addresses" list into distinct email strings. 2. For EACH distinct email address, generate an exact 1:1 clone of the "Original Template Settings" JSON object. 3. EXACT MAPPING (CRITICAL): You must retain EVERY SINGLE key-value pair from the Original Template Settings. Do NOT summarize. Do NOT drop any fields. Keys like 'saveEmailHeaders', 'isPermsetControlled', and 'createTask' MUST be preserved exactly as they appear in the original template. 4. OVERRIDE 'emailAddress': Update the 'emailAddress' property strictly to the current target email address. 5. GENERATE 'routingName': Create a unique 'routingName' based on the email address (e.g., if the email is "support.user@acme.com", routingName becomes "EQ_SupportU"). 6. STRIP SYSTEM FIELDS: You must completely remove the 'isVerified' and 'emailServicesAddress' properties if they exist in the template. Do not output them.
OUTPUT FORMAT: - Output ONLY a valid JSON Array of objects containing the new configurations. - Example structure: [{"addressType": "EmailToCase", "emailAddress": "one@gen.com", ... }, {"addressType": "EmailToCase", ... }] - DO NOT wrap the output in markdown formatting (do NOT use ```json or ```). - DO NOT include any conversational text, greetings, or explanations. Output the raw JSON array string only.
